stem: A traffic analysis dataset contains 80 observations of page load time. The data is believed to come from a mixture of two populations: mobile users (mean ≈ 30.2 ms, SD ≈ 3.4) and desktop users (mean ≈ 64.9 ms, SD ≈ 15.9). The proportion of mobile users in the sample is uncertain, estimated between 36% and 53%. The observed overall mean is 49.3 ms with SD = 21.0. Estimate the true population mean of page load time, accounting for the uncertainty in the mixing proportion.

ground_truth.answer: Mixture mean = w×30.2 + (1-w)×64.9. As w varies from 0.36 to 0.53, the mixture mean ranges from 46.5 to 52.4. With sampling variability, CI = [41.9, 57.0]. Point estimate at w=0.45: 49.3 ms.

solution_steps: Mixture mean formula: μ_mix = w × μ_A + (1-w) × μ_B.
At w=0.36: μ_mix = 52.4 (more of desktop users).
At w=0.53: μ_mix = 46.5 (more of mobile users).
Combined interval [41.9, 57.0] accounts for both mixing uncertainty and sampling variability.
common_failure_modes: Using the simple average of the two component means without weighting
Treating the mixing proportion as known instead of uncertain
Ignoring that the overall SD (21.0) is inflated by the between-component variation
